steps.title: Interpret the definition of tall
steps.content: A horticulturist classifies a plant as tall if its typical fully-grown height satisfies h > 120 cm. Any inequality that represents a tall species must have all possible heights above 120.

steps.title: Screen each choice by its lower bound
steps.content: For a compound inequality a < h < b, the smallest possible height is just above a. We need a ≥ 120 for every height to exceed 120.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $127 \lt h \lt 166$: Lower bound is 127 > 120, so every h in this range exceeds 120. Correct
  • Choice B — $96 \lt h \lt 120$: Lower bound is 96 < 120 and upper bound is exactly 120; no value in this range exceeds 120. Incorrect
  • Choice C — $54 \lt h \lt 98$: All values are well below 120. Incorrect
  • Choice D — $25 \lt h \lt 93$: All values are well below 120. Incorrect
That matches Choice A — $127 \lt h \lt 166$.
